children.heading: Appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver
children.label: a Appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal or State law, the Director may appoint the Agency as conservator or receiver for a regulated entity in the manner provided under paragraph (2) or (4). All references to the conservator or receiver under this section are references to the Agency acting as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Discretionary appointment
children.label: 2 Discretionary appointment
children.text: The Agency may, at the discretion of the Director, be appointed conservator or receiver for the purpose of reorganizing, rehabilitating, or winding up the affairs of a regulated entity.

children.heading: Grounds for discretionary appointment of conservator or receiver
children.label: 3 Grounds for discretionary appointment of conservator or receiver

children.heading: Assets insufficient for obligations
children.label: A Assets insufficient for obligations
children.text: The assets of the regulated entity are less than the obligations of the regulated entity to its creditors and others.

children.label: i
children.text: any violation of any provision of Federal or State law; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any unsafe or unsound practice.

children.heading: Unsafe or unsound condition
children.label: C Unsafe or unsound condition
children.text: An unsafe or unsound condition to transact business.

children.heading: Cease and desist orders
children.label: D Cease and desist orders
children.text: Any willful violation of a cease and desist order that has become final.

children.heading: Concealment
children.label: E Concealment
children.text: Any concealment of the books, papers, records, or assets of the regulated entity, or any refusal to submit the books, papers, records, or affairs of the regulated entity, for inspection to any examiner or to any lawful agent of the Director.

children.heading: Inability to meet obligations
children.label: F Inability to meet obligations
children.text: The regulated entity is likely to be unable to pay its obligations or meet the demands of its creditors in the normal course of business.

children.heading: Losses
children.label: G Losses
children.text: The regulated entity has incurred or is likely to incur losses that will deplete all or substantially all of its capital, and there is no reasonable prospect for the regulated entity to become adequately capitalized (as defined in ).section 4614(a)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: cause insolvency or substantial dissipation of assets or earnings; or

children.label: ii
children.text: weaken the condition of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Consent
children.label: I Consent
children.text: The regulated entity, by resolution of its board of directors or its shareholders or members, consents to the appointment.

children.label: i
children.text: has no reasonable prospect of becoming adequately capitalized;

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to a regulated entity; orsection 4615(a)(1) of this title

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a significantly undercapitalized regulated entity;section 4616(a)(1) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: fails to submit a capital restoration plan acceptable to the Agency within the time prescribed under ; orsection 4622 of this title

children.label: iv
children.text: materially fails to implement a capital restoration plan submitted and accepted under .section 4622 of this title

children.heading: Critical undercapitalization
children.label: K Critical undercapitalization
children.text: The regulated entity is critically undercapitalized, as defined in .section 4614(a)(4) of this title

children.heading: Money laundering
children.label: L Money laundering
children.text: The Attorney General notifies the Director in writing that the regulated entity has been found guilty of a criminal offense under section 1956 or 1957 of title 18 or section 5322 or 5324 of title 31.

children.label: i
children.text: the assets of the regulated entity are, and during the preceding 60 calendar days have been, less than the obligations of the regulated entity to its creditors and others; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the regulated entity is not, and during the preceding 60 calendar days has not been, generally paying the debts of the regulated entity (other than debts that are the subject of a bona fide dispute) as such debts become due.

children.heading: Periodic determination required for critically undercapitalized regulated entity
children.label: B Periodic determination required for critically undercapitalized regulated entity
i
children.text: not later than 30 calendar days after the regulated entity initially becomes critically undercapitalized; and

children.label: ii
children.text: at least once during each succeeding 30-calendar day period.

children.heading: Determination not required if receivership already in place
children.label: C Determination not required if receivership already in place
children.text: Subparagraph (B) does not apply with respect to a regulated entity in any period during which the Agency serves as receiver for the regulated entity.

children.heading: Receivership terminates conservatorship
children.label: D Receivership terminates conservatorship
children.text: The appointment of the Agency as receiver of a regulated entity under this section shall immediately terminate any conservatorship established for the regulated entity under this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the Agency is appointed conservator or receiver under this section, the regulated entity may, within 30 days of such appointment, bring an action in the United States district court for the judicial district in which the home office of such regulated entity is located, or in the United States District Court for the District of Columbia, for an order requiring the Agency to remove itself as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Review
children.label: B Review
children.text: Upon the filing of an action under subparagraph (A), the court shall, upon the merits, dismiss such action or direct the Agency to remove itself as such conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Directors not liable for acquiescing in appointment of conservator or receiver
children.label: 6 Directors not liable for acquiescing in appointment of conservator or receiver
children.text: The members of the board of directors of a regulated entity shall not be liable to the shareholders or creditors of the regulated entity for acquiescing in or consenting in good faith to the appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver for that regulated entity.

children.heading: Agency not subject to any other Federal agency
children.label: 7 Agency not subject to any other Federal agency
children.text: When acting as conservator or receiver, the Agency shall not be subject to the direction or supervision of any other agency of the United States or any State in the exercise of the rights, powers, and privileges of the Agency.

children.heading: Powers and duties of the Agency as conservator or receiver
children.label: b Powers and duties of the Agency as conservator or receiver

children.heading: Rulemaking authority of the agency
children.label: 1 Rulemaking authority of the agency
children.text: The Agency may prescribe such regulations as the Agency determines to be appropriate regarding the conduct of conservatorships or receiverships.

children.label: i
children.text: all rights, titles, powers, and privileges of the regulated entity, and of any stockholder, officer, or director of such regulated entity with respect to the regulated entity and the assets of the regulated entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: title to the books, records, and assets of any other legal custodian of such regulated entity.

children.label: i
children.text: take over the assets of and operate the regulated entity with all the powers of the shareholders, the directors, and the officers of the regulated entity and conduct all business of the regulated entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: collect all obligations and money due the regulated entity;

children.label: iii
children.text: perform all functions of the regulated entity in the name of the regulated entity which are consistent with the appointment as conservator or receiver;

children.label: iv
children.text: preserve and conserve the assets and property of the regulated entity; and

children.label: v
children.text: provide by contract for assistance in fulfilling any function, activity, action, or duty of the Agency as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Functions of officers, directors, and shareholders of a regulated entity
children.label: C Functions of officers, directors, and shareholders of a regulated entity
children.text: The Agency may, by regulation or order, provide for the exercise of any function by any stockholder, director, or officer of any regulated entity for which the Agency has been named conservator or receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: necessary to put the regulated entity in a sound and solvent condition; and

children.label: ii
children.text: appropriate to carry on the business of the regulated entity and preserve and conserve the assets and property of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Additional powers as receiver
children.label: E Additional powers as receiver
children.text: In any case in which the Agency is acting as receiver, the Agency shall place the regulated entity in liquidation and proceed to realize upon the assets of the regulated entity in such manner as the Agency deems appropriate, including through the sale of assets, the transfer of assets to a limited-life regulated entity established under subsection (i), or the exercise of any other rights or privileges granted to the Agency under this paragraph.

children.heading: Organization of new enterprise
children.label: F Organization of new enterprise
children.text: The Agency may, as receiver for an enterprise, organize a successor enterprise that will operate pursuant to subsection (i).

children.heading: Transfer or sale of assets and liabilities
children.label: G Transfer or sale of assets and liabilities
children.text: The Agency may, as conservator or receiver, transfer or sell any asset or liability of the regulated entity in default, and may do so without any approval, assignment, or consent with respect to such transfer or sale.

children.heading: Payment of valid obligations
children.label: H Payment of valid obligations
children.text: The Agency, as conservator or receiver, shall, to the extent of proceeds realized from the performance of contracts or sale of the assets of a regulated entity, pay all valid obligations of the regulated entity that are due and payable at the time of the appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver, in accordance with the prescriptions and limitations of this section.

children.heading: Agency authority
children.label: I Agency authority
children.text: The Agency may, as conservator or receiver, and for purposes of carrying out any power, authority, or duty with respect to a regulated entity (including determining any claim against the regulated entity and determining and realizing upon any asset of any person in the course of collecting money due the regulated entity), exercise any power established under .section 4588 of this title

children.heading: Applicability of law
children.label: II Applicability of law
children.text: The provisions of shall apply with respect to the exercise of any power under this subparagraph, in the same manner as such provisions apply under that section.section 4588 of this title

children.heading: Subpoena
children.label: ii Subpoena
children.text: A subpoena or subpoena duces tecum may be issued under clause (i) only by, or with the written approval of, the Director, or the designee of the Director.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: iii Rule of construction
children.text: This subsection shall not be construed to limit any rights that the Agency, in any capacity, might otherwise have under section 4517 or 4639 of this title.

children.label: i
children.text: exercise all powers and authorities specifically granted to conservators or receivers, respectively, under this section, and such incidental powers as shall be necessary to carry out such powers; and

children.label: ii
children.text: take any action authorized by this section, which the Agency determines is in the best interests of the regulated entity or the Agency.

children.heading: Shareholders and creditors of failed regulated entity
children.label: i Shareholders and creditors of failed regulated entity
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the appointment of the Agency as receiver for a regulated entity pursuant to paragraph (2) or (4) of subsection (a) and its succession, by operation of law, to the rights, titles, powers, and privileges described in subsection (b)(2)(A) shall terminate all rights and claims that the stockholders and creditors of the regulated entity may have against the assets or charter of the regulated entity or the Agency arising as a result of their status as stockholders or creditors, except for their right to payment, resolution, or other satisfaction of their claims, as permitted under subsections (b)(9), (c), and (e).

children.heading: Assets of regulated entity
children.label: ii Assets of regulated entity
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, for purposes of this section, the charter of a regulated entity shall not be considered an asset of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Authority of receiver to determine claims
children.label: 3 Authority of receiver to determine claims

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Agency may, as receiver, determine claims in accordance with the requirements of this subsection and any regulations prescribed under paragraph (4).

children.label: i
children.text: promptly publish a notice to the creditors of the regulated entity to present their claims, together with proof, to the receiver by a date specified in the notice which shall be not less than 90 days after the date of publication of such notice; and

children.label: ii
children.text: republish such notice approximately 1 month and 2 months, respectively, after the date of publication under clause (i).

children.label: i
children.text: at the last address of the creditor appearing in such books; or

children.label: ii
children.text: upon discovery of the name and address of a claimant not appearing on the books of the regulated entity, within 30 days after the discovery of such name and address.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims
children.label: 4 Rulemaking authority relating to determination of claims
children.text: Subject to subsection (c), the Director may prescribe regulations regarding the allowance or disallowance of claims by the receiver and providing for administrative determination of claims and review of such determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Before the end of the 180-day period beginning on the date on which any claim against a regulated entity is filed with the Agency as receiver, the Agency shall determine whether to allow or disallow the claim and shall notify the claimant of any determination with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Extension of time
children.label: ii Extension of time
children.text: The period described in clause (i) may be extended by a written agreement between the claimant and the Agency.

children.label: I
children.text: on the books of the regulated entity;

children.label: II
children.text: in the claim filed by the claimant; or

children.label: III
children.text: in documents submitted in proof of the claim.

children.heading: Contents of notice of disallowance
children.label: iv Contents of notice of disallowance
I
children.text: a statement of each reason for the disallowance; and

children.label: II
children.text: the procedures available for obtaining agency review of the determination to disallow the claim or judicial determination of the claim.

children.heading: Allowance of proven claim
children.label: B Allowance of proven claim
children.text: The receiver shall allow any claim received on or before the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i) by the receiver from any claimant which is proved to the satisfaction of the receiver.

children.heading: Disallowance of claims filed after filing period
children.label: C Disallowance of claims filed after filing period
children.text: Claims filed after the date specified in the notice published under paragraph (3)(B)(i), or the date specified under paragraph (3)(C), shall be disallowed and such disallowance shall be final.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The receiver may disallow any portion of any claim by a creditor or claim of security, preference, or priority which is not proved to the satisfaction of the receiver.

children.heading: Payments to less than fully secured creditors
children.label: ii Payments to less than fully secured creditors
I
children.text: may treat the portion of such claim which exceeds an amount equal to the fair market value of such property or other asset as an unsecured claim against the regulated entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: may not make any payment with respect to such unsecured portion of the claim, other than in connection with the disposition of all claims of unsecured creditors of the regulated entity.

children.label: I
children.text: any extension of credit from any Federal Reserve Bank, Federal Home Loan Bank, or the United States Treasury; or

children.label: II
children.text: any security interest in the assets of the regulated entity securing any such extension of credit.

children.heading: No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.label: E No judicial review of determination pursuant to subparagraph (D)
children.text: No court may review the determination of the Agency under subparagraph (D) to disallow a claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the receiver shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (10), the filing of a claim with the receiver shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action which was filed before the date of the appointment of the receiver, subject to the determination of claims by the receiver.

children.heading: Provision for judicial determination of claims
children.label: 6 Provision for judicial determination of claims
i
children.text: the end of the period described in paragraph (5)(A)(i) with respect to any claim against a regulated entity for which the Agency is receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of any notice of disallowance of such claim pursuant to paragraph (5)(A)(i).

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: B Statute of limitations
children.text: A claim shall be deemed to be disallowed (other than any portion of such claim which was allowed by the receiver), and such disallowance shall be final, and the claimant shall have no further rights or remedies with respect to such claim, if the claimant fails, before the end of the 60-day period described under subparagraph (A), to file suit on such claim (or continue an action commenced before the appointment of the receiver).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Agency shall establish such alternative dispute resolution processes as may be appropriate for the resolution of claims filed under paragraph (5)(A)(i).

children.heading: Criteria
children.label: ii Criteria
children.text: In establishing alternative dispute resolution processes, the Agency shall strive for procedures which are expeditious, fair, independent, and low cost.

children.heading: Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.label: iii Voluntary binding or nonbinding procedures
children.text: The Agency may establish both binding and nonbinding processes under this subparagraph, which may be conducted by any government or private party. All parties, including the claimant and the Agency, must agree to the use of the process in a particular case.

children.heading: Consideration of incentives
children.label: B Consideration of incentives
children.text: The Agency shall seek to develop incentives for claimants to participate in the alternative dispute resolution process.

children.label: i
children.text: allege the existence of legally valid and enforceable or perfected security interests in assets of any regulated entity for which the Agency has been appointed receiver; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allege that irreparable injury will occur if the routine claims procedure is followed.

children.label: I
children.text: whether to allow or disallow such claim; or

children.label: II
children.text: whether such claim should be determined pursuant to the procedures established under paragraph (5); and

children.label: ii
children.text: notify the claimant of the determination, and if the claim is disallowed, provide a statement of each reason for the disallowance and the procedure for obtaining agency review or judicial determination.

children.heading: Period for filing or renewing suit
children.label: C Period for filing or renewing suit
i
children.text: the end of the 90-day period beginning on the date of the filing of a request for expedited relief; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the Agency denies the claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitations
children.label: D Statute of limitations
children.text: If an action described under subparagraph (C) is not filed, or the motion to renew a previously filed suit is not made, before the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which such action or motion may be filed under subparagraph (B), the claim shall be deemed to be disallowed as of the end of such period (other than any portion of such claim which was allowed by the receiver), such disallowance shall be final, and the claimant shall have no further rights or remedies with respect to such claim.

children.heading: Statute of limitation tolled
children.label: i Statute of limitation tolled
children.text: For purposes of any applicable statute of limitations, the filing of a claim with the receiver shall constitute a commencement of an action.

children.heading: No prejudice to other actions
children.label: ii No prejudice to other actions
children.text: Subject to paragraph (10), the filing of a claim with the receiver shall not prejudice any right of the claimant to continue any action that was filed before the appointment of the receiver, subject to the determination of claims by the receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: allowed by the receiver;

children.label: ii
children.text: approved by the Agency pursuant to a final determination pursuant to paragraph (7) or (8); or

children.label: iii
children.text: determined by the final judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction.

children.heading: Agreements against the interest of the Agency
children.label: B Agreements against the interest of the Agency
children.text: No agreement that tends to diminish or defeat the interest of the Agency in any asset acquired by the Agency as receiver under this section shall be valid against the Agency unless such agreement is in writing and executed by an authorized officer or representative of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Payment of dividends on claims
children.label: C Payment of dividends on claims
children.text: The receiver may, in the sole discretion of the receiver, pay from the assets of the regulated entity dividends on proved claims at any time, and no liability shall attach to the Agency by reason of any such payment, for failure to pay dividends to a claimant whose claim is not proved at the time of any such payment.

children.heading: Rulemaking authority of the Director
children.label: D Rulemaking authority of the Director
children.text: The Director may prescribe such rules, including definitions of terms, as the Director deems appropriate to establish a single uniform interest rate for, or to make payments of post-insolvency interest to creditors holding proven claims against the receivership estates of the regulated entity, following satisfaction by the receiver of the principal amount of all creditor claims.

children.label: i
children.text: 45 days, in the case of any conservator; and

children.label: ii
children.text: 90 days, in the case of any receiver.

children.heading: Grant of stay by all courts required
children.label: B Grant of stay by all courts required
children.text: Upon receipt of a request by the conservator or receiver under subparagraph (A) for a stay of any judicial action or proceeding in any court with jurisdiction of such action or proceeding, the court shall grant such stay as to all parties.

children.heading: Prior final adjudication
children.label: A Prior final adjudication
children.text: The Agency shall abide by any final unappealable judgment of any court of competent jurisdiction which was rendered before the appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver.

children.heading: Rights and remedies of conservator or receiver
children.label: B Rights and remedies of conservator or receiver
i
children.text: shall have all of the rights and remedies available to the regulated entity (before the appointment of such conservator or receiver) and the Agency, including removal to Federal court and all appellate rights; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be required to post any bond in order to pursue such remedies.

children.heading: No attachment or execution
children.label: C No attachment or execution
children.text: No attachment or execution may issue by any court upon assets in the possession of the receiver, or upon the charter, of a regulated entity for which the Agency has been appointed receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: any claim or action for payment from, or any action seeking a determination of rights with respect to, the assets or charter of any regulated entity for which the Agency has been appointed receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any claim relating to any act or omission of such regulated entity or the Agency as receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: maximizes the net present value return from the sale or disposition of such assets;

children.label: ii
children.text: minimizes the amount of any loss realized in the resolution of cases; and

children.label: iii
children.text: ensures adequate competition and fair and consistent treatment of offerors.

children.heading: Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or receiver
children.label: 12 Statute of limitations for actions brought by conservator or receiver
I
children.text: the 6-year period beginning on the date on which the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law; and

children.label: I
children.text: the 3-year period beginning on the date on which the claim accrues; or

children.label: II
children.text: the period applicable under State law.

children.heading: Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
children.label: B Determination of the date on which a claim accrues
i
children.text: the date of the appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the cause of action accrues.

children.heading: Revival of expired state causes of action
children.label: 13 Revival of expired state causes of action

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any tort claim described under clause (ii) for which the statute of limitations applicable under State law with respect to such claim has expired not more than 5 years before the appointment of the Agency as conservator or receiver, the Agency may bring an action as conservator or receiver on such claim without regard to the expiration of the statute of limitations applicable under State law.

children.heading: Claims described
children.label: B Claims described
children.text: A tort claim referred to under clause (i) is a claim arising from fraud, intentional misconduct resulting in unjust enrichment, or intentional misconduct resulting in substantial loss to the regulated entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Agency as conservator or receiver shall, consistent with the accounting and reporting practices and procedures established by the Agency, maintain a full accounting of each conservatorship and receivership or other disposition of a regulated entity in default.

children.heading: Annual accounting or report
children.label: B Annual accounting or report
children.text: With respect to each conservatorship or receivership, the Agency shall make an annual accounting or report available to the Board, the Comptroller General of the United States, the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs of the Senate, and the Committee on Financial Services of the House of Representatives.

children.heading: Availability of reports
children.label: C Availability of reports
children.text: Any report prepared under subparagraph (B) shall be made available by the Agency upon request to any shareholder of a regulated entity or any member of the public.

children.heading: Recordkeeping requirement
children.label: D Recordkeeping requirement
children.text: After the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date on which the conservatorship or receivership is terminated by the Director, the Agency may destroy any records of such regulated entity which the Agency, in the discretion of the Agency, determines to be unnecessary, unless directed not to do so by a court of competent jurisdiction or governmental agency, or prohibited by law.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Agency, as conservator or receiver, may avoid a transfer of any interest of an entity-affiliated party, or any person determined by the conservator or receiver to be a debtor of the regulated entity, in property, or any obligation incurred by such party or person, that was made within 5 years of the date on which the Agency was appointed conservator or receiver, if such party or person voluntarily or involuntarily made such transfer or incurred such liability with the intent to hinder, delay, or defraud the regulated entity, the Agency, the conservator, or receiver.

children.label: i
children.text: the initial transferee of such transfer or the entity-affiliated party or person for whose benefit such transfer was made; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate transferee of any such initial transferee.

children.heading: Rights of transferee or obligee
children.label: C Rights of transferee or obligee
i
children.text: any transferee that takes for value, including satisfaction or securing of a present or antecedent debt, in good faith; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any immediate or mediate good faith transferee of such transferee.

children.heading: Rights under this paragraph
children.label: D Rights under this paragraph
children.text: The rights under this paragraph of the conservator or receiver described under subparagraph (A) shall be superior to any rights of a trustee or any other party (other than any party which is a Federal agency) under title 11.

children.heading: Attachment of assets and other injunctive relief
children.label: 16 Attachment of assets and other injunctive relief
children.text: Subject to paragraph (17), any court of competent jurisdiction may, at the request of the conservator or receiver, issue an order in accordance with rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, including an order placing the assets of any person designated by the conservator or receiver under the control of the court, and appointing a trustee to hold such assets.

children.heading: Standards of proof
children.label: 17 Standards of proof
children.text: Rule 65 of the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure shall apply with respect to any proceeding under paragraph (16) without regard to the requirement of such rule that the applicant show that the injury, loss, or damage is irreparable and immediate.

children.heading: Treatment of claims arising from breach of contracts executed by the conservator or receiver
children.label: 18 Treatment of claims arising from breach of contracts executed by the conservator or receiver

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, any final and unappealable judgment for monetary damages entered against the conservator or receiver for the breach of an agreement executed or approved in writing by the conservator or receiver after the date of its appointment, shall be paid as an administrative expense of the conservator or receiver.

children.heading: No limitation of power
children.label: B No limitation of power
children.text: Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed to limit the power of the conservator or receiver to exercise any rights under contract or law, including to terminate, breach, cancel, or otherwise discontinue such agreement.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: A Limitations
children.text: The rights of the conservator or receiver appointed under this section shall be subject to the limitations on the powers of a receiver under sections 4402 through 4407 of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any mortgage, pool of mortgages, or interest in a pool of mortgages held in trust, custodial, or agency capacity by a regulated entity for the benefit of any person other than the regulated entity shall not be available to satisfy the claims of creditors generally, except that nothing in this clause shall be construed to expand or otherwise affect the authority of any regulated entity.

children.heading: Holding of mortgages
children.label: ii Holding of mortgages
children.text: Any mortgage, pool of mortgages, or interest in a pool of mortgages described in clause (i) shall be held by the conservator or receiver appointed under this section for the beneficial owners of such mortgage, pool of mortgages, or interest in accordance with the terms of the agreement creating such trust, custodial, or other agency arrangement.

children.heading: Liability of conservator or receiver
children.label: iii Liability of conservator or receiver
children.text: The liability of the conservator or receiver appointed under this section for damages shall, in the case of any contingent or unliquidated claim relating to the mortgages held in trust, be estimated in accordance with the regulations of the Director.

children.heading: Priority of expenses and unsecured claims
children.label: c Priority of expenses and unsecured claims
A
children.text: Administrative expenses of the receiver.

children.label: B
children.text: Any other general or senior liability of the regulated entity (which is not a liability described under subparagraph (C) or (D).2So in original. A second closing parenthesis probably should precede the period.2

children.label: C
children.text: Any obligation subordinated to general creditors (which is not an obligation described under subparagraph (D)).

children.label: D
children.text: Any obligation to shareholders or members arising as a result of their status as shareholder or members.

children.label: A
children.text: the Director determines that such action is necessary to maximize the value of the assets of the regulated entity, to maximize the present value return from the sale or other disposition of the assets of the regulated entity, or to minimize the amount of any loss realized upon the sale or other disposition of the assets of the regulated entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: all creditors that are similarly situated under paragraph (1) receive not less than the amount provided in subsection (e)(2).

children.label: A
children.text: the actual, necessary costs and expenses incurred by the receiver in preserving the assets of a failed regulated entity or liquidating or otherwise resolving the affairs of a failed regulated entity; and

children.label: B
children.text: any obligations that the receiver determines are necessary and appropriate to facilitate the smooth and orderly liquidation or other resolution of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or receiver
children.label: d Provisions relating to contracts entered into before appointment of conservator or receiver
A
children.text: to which such regulated entity is a party;

children.label: B
children.text: the performance of which the conservator or receiver, in its sole discretion, determines to be burdensome; and

children.label: C
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation of which the conservator or receiver determines, in its sole discretion, will promote the orderly administration of the affairs of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Timing of repudiation
children.label: 2 Timing of repudiation
children.text: The conservator or receiver shall determine whether or not to exercise the rights of repudiation under this subsection within a reasonable period following such appointment.

children.label: i
children.text: limited to actual direct compensatory damages; and

children.label: I
children.text: the date of the appointment of the conservator or receiver; or

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any contract or agreement referred to in paragraph (8), the date of the disaffirmance or repudiation of such contract or agreement.

children.heading: No liability for other damages
children.label: B No liability for other damages
i
children.text: punitive or exemplary damages;

children.label: ii
children.text: damages for lost profits or opportunity; or

children.label: iii
children.text: damages for pain and suffering.

children.heading: Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
children.label: C Measure of damages for repudiation of financial contracts
i
children.text: deemed to include normal and reasonable costs of cover or other reasonable measures of damages utilized in the industries for such contract and agreement claims; and

children.label: ii
children.text: paid in accordance with this subsection and subsection (e), except as otherwise specifically provided in this section.

children.heading: Leases under which the regulated entity is the lessee
children.label: 4 Leases under which the regulated entity is the lessee

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If the conservator or receiver disaffirms or repudiates a lease under which the regulated entity was the lessee, the conservator or receiver shall not be liable for any damages (other than damages determined under subparagraph (B)) for the disaffirmance or repudiation of such lease.

children.label: I
children.text: the notice of disaffirmance or repudiation is mailed; or

children.label: II
children.text: the disaffirmance or repudiation becomes effective, unless the lessor is in default or breach of the terms of the lease;

children.label: ii
children.text: have no claim for damages under any acceleration clause or other penalty provision in the lease; and

children.label: iii
children.text: have a claim for any unpaid rent, subject to all appropriate offsets and defenses, due as of the date of the appointment, which shall be paid in accordance with this subsection and subsection (e).

children.heading: Leases under which the regulated entity is the lessor
children.label: 5 Leases under which the regulated entity is the lessor
i
children.text: treat the lease as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of the leasehold interest for the balance of the term of the lease, unless the lessee defaults under the terms of the lease after the date of such repudiation.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to lessee remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to pay the contractual rent pursuant to the terms of the lease after the date of the repudiation of such lease; and

children.label: II
children.text: may offset against any rent payment which accrues after the date of the repudiation of the lease, and any damages which accrue after such date due to the nonperformance of any obligation of the regulated entity under the lease after such date; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the conservator or receiver shall not be liable to the lessee for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation, other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II).

children.heading: Contracts for the sale of real property
children.label: 6 Contracts for the sale of real property
i
children.text: treat the contract as terminated by such repudiation; or

children.label: ii
children.text: remain in possession of such real property.

children.heading: Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
children.label: B Provisions applicable to purchaser remaining in possession
I
children.text: shall continue to make all payments due under the contract after the date of the repudiation of the contract; and

children.label: II
children.text: may offset against any such payments any damages which accrue after such date due to the nonperformance (after such date) of any obligation of the regulated entity under the contract; and

children.label: I
children.text: not be liable to the purchaser for any damages arising after such date as a result of the repudiation, other than the amount of any offset allowed under clause (i)(II);

children.label: II
children.text: deliver title to the purchaser in accordance with the provisions of the contract; and

children.label: III
children.text: have no obligation under the contract other than the performance required under subclause (II).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: No provision of this paragraph shall be construed as limiting the right of the conservator or receiver to assign the contract described under subparagraph (A), and sell the property subject to the contract and the provisions of this paragraph.

children.heading: No liability after assignment and sale
children.label: ii No liability after assignment and sale
children.text: If an assignment and sale described under clause (i) is consummated, the conservator or receiver shall have no further liability under the contract described under subparagraph (A), or with respect to the real property which was the subject of such contract.

children.label: i
children.text: a claim to be paid in accordance with subsections (b) and (e); and

children.label: ii
children.text: deemed to have arisen as of the date on which the conservator or receiver was appointed.

children.heading: Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
children.label: B Services performed after appointment and prior to repudiation
i
children.text: the other party shall be paid under the terms of the contract for the services performed; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of such payment shall be treated as an administrative expense of the conservatorship or receivership.

children.heading: Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.label: C Acceptance of performance no bar to subsequent repudiation
children.text: The acceptance by the conservator or receiver of services referred to under subparagraph (B) in connection with a contract described in such subparagraph shall not affect the right of the conservator or receiver to repudiate such contract under this section at any time after such performance.

children.heading: Rights of parties to contracts
children.label: A Rights of parties to contracts
i
children.text: any right of that person to cause the termination, liquidation, or acceleration of any qualified financial contract with a regulated entity that arises upon the appointment of the Agency as receiver for such regulated entity at any time after such appointment;

children.label: ii
children.text: any right under any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement relating to one or more qualified financial contracts; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any right to offset or net out any termination value, payment amount, or other transfer obligation arising under or in connection with 1 or more contracts and agreements described in clause (i), including any master agreement for such contracts or agreements.

children.heading: Applicability of other provisions
children.label: B Applicability of other provisions
children.text: Subsection (b)(10) shall apply in the case of any judicial action or proceeding brought against any receiver referred to under subparagraph (A), or the regulated entity for which such receiver was appointed, by any party to a contract or agreement described under subparagraph (A)(i) with such regulated entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (11), or any other provision of Federal or State law relating to the avoidance of preferential or fraudulent transfers, the Agency, whether acting as such or as conservator or receiver of a regulated entity, may not avoid any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with a regulated entity.

children.heading: Exception for certain transfers
children.label: ii Exception for certain transfers
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any transfer of money or other property in connection with any qualified financial contract with a regulated entity if the Agency determines that the transferee had actual intent to hinder, delay, or defraud such regulated entity, the creditors of such regulated entity, or any conservator or receiver appointed for such regulated entity.

children.heading: Certain contracts and agreements defined
children.label: D Certain contracts and agreements defined

children.heading: Qualified financial contract
children.label: i Qualified financial contract
children.text: The term “qualified financial contract” means any securities contract, commodity contract, forward contract, repurchase agreement, swap agreement, and any similar agreement that the Agency determines by regulation, resolution, or order to be a qualified financial contract for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: I
children.text: means a contract for the purchase, sale, or loan of a security, a certificate of deposit, a mortgage loan, or any interest in a mortgage loan, a group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or any option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option, and including any repurchase or reverse repurchase transaction on any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option;

children.label: II
children.text: does not include any purchase, sale, or repurchase obligation under a participation in a commercial mortgage loan, unless the Agency determines by regulation, resolution, or order to include any such agreement within the meaning of such term;

children.label: III
children.text: means any option entered into on a national securities exchange relating to foreign currencies;

children.label: IV
children.text: means the guarantee by or to any securities clearing agency of any settlement of cash, securities, certificates of deposit, mortgage loans or interests therein, group or index of securities, certificates of deposit, or mortgage loans or interests therein (including any interest therein or based on the value thereof) or option on any of the foregoing, including any option to purchase or sell any such security, certificate of deposit, mortgage loan, interest, group or index, or option;

children.label: V
children.text: means any margin loan;

children.label: VI
children.text: means any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: VII
children.text: means any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: VIII
children.text: means any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: IX
children.text: means a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a securities contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a securities contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII); and

children.label: X
children.text: means any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to a futures commission merchant, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade;

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to a foreign futures commission merchant, a foreign future;

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to a leverage transaction merchant, a leverage transaction;

children.label: IV
children.text: with respect to a clearing organization, a contract for the purchase or sale of a commodity for future delivery on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization, or commodity option traded on, or subject to the rules of, a contract market or board of trade that is cleared by such clearing organization;

children.label: V
children.text: with respect to a commodity options dealer, a commodity option;

children.label: VI
children.text: any other agreement or transaction that is similar to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: VII
children.text: any combination of the agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: VIII
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: IX
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a commodity contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a commodity contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), (V), (VI), (VII), or (VIII); or

children.label: X
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause, including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause.

children.label: I
children.text: a contract (other than a commodity contract) for the purchase, sale, or transfer of a commodity or any similar good, article, service, right, or interest which is presently or in the future becomes the subject of dealing in the forward contract trade, or product or byproduct thereof, with a maturity date more than 2 days after the date on which the contract is entered into, including a repurchase transaction, reverse repurchase transaction, consignment, lease, swap, hedge transaction, deposit, loan, option, allocated transaction, unallocated transaction, or any other similar agreement;

children.label: II
children.text: any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in subclauses (I) and (III);

children.label: III
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I) or (II);

children.label: IV
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclauses (I), (II), or (III), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a forward contract under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a forward contract under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), or (III); or

children.label: V
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: means an agreement, including related terms, which provides for the transfer of one or more certificates of deposit, mortgage-related securities (as such term is defined in ), mortgage loans, interests in mortgage-related securities or mortgage loans, eligible bankers’ acceptances, qualified foreign government securities (defined for purposes of this clause as a security that is a direct obligation of, or that is fully guaranteed by, the central government of a member of the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development, as determined by regulation or order adopted by the appropriate Federal banking authority), or securities that are direct obligations of, or that are fully guaranteed by, the United States or any agency of the United States against the transfer of funds by the transferee of such certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests with a simultaneous agreement by such transferee to transfer to the transferor thereof certificates of deposit, eligible bankers’ acceptances, securities, mortgage loans, or interests as described above, at a date certain not later than 1 year after such transfers or on demand, against the transfer of funds, or any other similar agreement;section 78c of title 15

children.label: II
children.text: does not include any repurchase obligation under a participation in a commercial mortgage loan, unless the Agency determines by regulation, resolution, or order to include any such participation within the meaning of such term;

children.label: III
children.text: means any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in subclauses (I) and (IV);

children.label: IV
children.text: means any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I) or (III);

children.label: V
children.text: means a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), or (IV), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement provides for an agreement or transaction that is not a repurchase agreement under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a repurchase agreement under this subclause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (III), or (IV); and

children.label: VI
children.text: means any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (III), (IV), or (V), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.label: I
children.text: any agreement, including the terms and conditions incorporated by reference in any such agreement, which is an interest rate swap, option, future, or forward agreement, including a rate floor, rate cap, rate collar, cross-currency rate swap, and basis swap; a spot, same day-tomorrow, tomorrow-next, forward, or other foreign exchange or precious metals agreement; a currency swap, option, future, or forward agreement; an equity index or equity swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a debt index or debt swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a total return, credit spread or credit swap, option, future, or forward agreement; a commodity index or commodity swap, option, future, or forward agreement; or a weather swap, weather derivative, or weather option;

children.label: II
children.text: any agreement or transaction that is similar to any other agreement or transaction referred to in this clause and that is of a type that has been, is presently, or in the future becomes, the subject of recurrent dealings in the swap markets (including terms and conditions incorporated by reference in such agreement) and that is a forward, swap, future, or option on one or more rates, currencies, commodities, equity securities or other equity instruments, debt securities or other debt instruments, quantitative measures associated with an occurrence, extent of an occurrence, or contingency associated with a financial, commercial, or economic consequence, or economic or financial indices or measures of economic or financial risk or value;

children.label: III
children.text: any combination of agreements or transactions referred to in this clause;

children.label: IV
children.text: any option to enter into any agreement or transaction referred to in this clause;

children.label: V
children.text: a master agreement that provides for an agreement or transaction referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV), together with all supplements to any such master agreement, without regard to whether the master agreement contains an agreement or transaction that is not a swap agreement under this clause, except that the master agreement shall be considered to be a swap agreement under this clause only with respect to each agreement or transaction under the master agreement that is referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), or (IV); and

children.label: VI
children.text: any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement related to any agreements or transactions referred to in subclause (I), (II), (III), (IV), or (V), including any guarantee or reimbursement obligation in connection with any agreement or transaction referred to in any such subclause.

children.heading: Treatment of master agreement as one agreement
children.label: vii Treatment of master agreement as one agreement
children.text: Any master agreement for any contract or agreement described in any preceding clause of this subparagraph (or any master agreement for such master agreement or agreements), together with all supplements to such master agreement, shall be treated as a single agreement and a single qualified financial contract. If a master agreement contains provisions relating to agreements or transactions that are not themselves qualified financial contracts, the master agreement shall be deemed to be a qualified financial contract only with respect to those transactions that are themselves qualified financial contracts.

children.heading: Transfer
children.label: viii Transfer
children.text: The term “transfer” means every mode, direct or indirect, absolute or conditional, voluntary or involuntary, of disposing of or parting with property or with an interest in property, including retention of title as a security interest and foreclosure of the equity of redemption of the regulated entity.

children.heading: Certain protections in event of appointment of conservator
children.label: E Certain protections in event of appointment of conservator
i
children.text: any right such person has to cause the termination, liquidation, or acceleration of any qualified financial contract with a regulated entity in a conservatorship based upon a default under such financial contract which is enforceable under applicable noninsolvency law;

children.label: ii
children.text: any right under any security agreement or arrangement or other credit enhancement relating to 1 or more such qualified financial contracts; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any right to offset or net out any termination values, payment amounts, or other transfer obligations arising under or in connection with such qualified financial contracts.

children.heading: Clarification
children.label: F Clarification
children.text: No provision of law shall be construed as limiting the right or power of the Agency, or authorizing any court or agency to limit or delay in any manner, the right or power of the Agency to transfer any qualified financial contract in accordance with paragraphs (9) and (10), or to disaffirm or repudiate any such contract in accordance with subsection (d)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding the provisions of subparagraphs (A) and (E), and sections 4403 and 4404 of this title, no walkaway clause shall be enforceable in a qualified financial contract of a regulated entity in default.

children.heading: Walkaway clause defined
children.label: ii Walkaway clause defined
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “walkaway clause” means a provision in a qualified financial contract that, after calculation of a value of a party’s position or an amount due to or from 1 of the parties in accordance with its terms upon termination, liquidation, or acceleration of the qualified financial contract, either does not create a payment obligation of a party or extinguishes a payment obligation of a party in whole or in part solely because of the status of such party as a nondefaulting party.

children.label: i
children.text: all qualified financial contracts between any person (or any affiliate of such person) and the regulated entity in default;

children.label: ii
children.text: all claims of such person (or any affiliate of such person) against such regulated entity under any such contract (other than any claim which, under the terms of any such contract, is subordinated to the claims of general unsecured creditors of such regulated entity);

children.label: iii
children.text: all claims of such regulated entity against such person (or any affiliate of such person) under any such contract; and

children.label: iv
children.text: all property securing, or any other credit enhancement for any contract described in clause (i), or any claim described in clause (ii) or (iii) under any such contract; or

children.label: B
children.text: transfer none of the financial contracts, claims, or property referred to under subparagraph (A) (with respect to such person and any affiliate of such person).

children.label: i
children.text: the conservator or receiver for a regulated entity in default makes any transfer of the assets and liabilities of such regulated entity; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such transfer includes any qualified financial contract.

children.label: I
children.text: until 5:00 p.m. (Eastern Standard Time) on the business day following the date of the appointment of the receiver; or

children.label: II
children.text: after the person has received notice that the contract has been transferred pursuant to paragraph (9)(A).

children.heading: Conservatorship
children.label: ii Conservatorship
children.text: A person who is a party to a qualified financial contract with a regulated entity may not exercise any right that such person has to terminate, liquidate, or net such contract under paragraph (8)(E) of this subsection or under section 4403 or 4404 of this title, solely by reason of or incidental to the appointment of a conservator for the regulated entity (or the insolvency or financial condition of the regulated entity for which the conservator has been appointed).

children.heading: Notice
children.label: iii Notice
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the conservator or receiver of a regulated entity shall be deemed to have notified a person who is a party to a qualified financial contract with such regulated entity, if the conservator or receiver has taken steps reasonably calculated to provide notice to such person by the time specified in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Business day defined
children.label: C Business day defined
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “business day” means any day other than any Saturday, Sunday, or any day on which either the New York Stock Exchange or the Federal Reserve Bank of New York is closed.

children.heading: Disaffirmance or repudiation of qualified financial contracts
children.label: 11 Disaffirmance or repudiation of qualified financial contracts
i
children.text: any person or any affiliate of such person; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the regulated entity in default; or

children.label: B
children.text: disaffirm or repudiate none of the qualified financial contracts referred to in subparagraph (A) (with respect to such person or any affiliate of such person).

children.heading: Certain security interests not avoidable
children.label: 12 Certain security interests not avoidable
children.text: No provision of this subsection shall be construed as permitting the avoidance of any legally enforceable or perfected security interest in any of the assets of any regulated entity, except where such an interest is taken in contemplation of the insolvency of the regulated entity, or with the intent to hinder, delay, or defraud the regulated entity or the creditors of such regulated entity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of a contract providing for termination, default, acceleration, or exercise of rights upon, or solely by reason of, insolvency or the appointment of, or the exercise of rights or powers by, a conservator or receiver, the conservator or receiver may enforce any contract, other than a contract for liability insurance for a director or officer, or a contract or a regulated entity bond, entered into by the regulated entity.

children.heading: Certain rights not affected
children.label: B Certain rights not affected
children.text: No provision of this paragraph may be construed as impairing or affecting any right of the conservator or receiver to enforce or recover under a liability insurance contract for an officer or director, or regulated entity bond under other applicable law.

children.label: I
children.text: 45 days after the date of appointment of a conservator; or

children.label: II
children.text: 90 days after the date of appointment of a receiver.

children.label: I
children.text: apply to a contract for liability insurance for an officer or director;

children.label: II
children.text: apply to the rights of parties to certain qualified financial contracts under subsection (d)(8); and

children.label: III
children.text: be construed as permitting the conservator or receiver to fail to comply with otherwise enforceable provisions of such contracts.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: 14 Savings clause
children.text: The meanings of terms used in this subsection are applicable for purposes of this subsection only, and shall not be construed or applied so as to challenge or affect the characterization, definition, or treatment of any similar terms under any other statute, regulation, or rule, including the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, the Legal Certainty for Bank Products Act of 2000 [ to 27f], the securities laws (as that term is defined in ), and the Commodity Exchange Act [ et seq.].7 U.S.C. 27section 78c(a)(47) of title 157 U.S.C. 1

children.heading: Exception for Federal Reserve and Federal Home Loan Banks
children.label: 15 Exception for Federal Reserve and Federal Home Loan Banks
A
children.text: any extension of credit from any Federal Home Loan Bank or Federal Reserve Bank to any regulated entity; or

children.label: B
children.text: any security interest in the assets of the regulated entity securing any such extension of credit.

children.heading: Valuation of claims in default
children.label: e Valuation of claims in default

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal law or the law of any State, and regardless of the method which the Agency determines to utilize with respect to a regulated entity in default or in danger of default, including transactions authorized under subsection (i), this subsection shall govern the rights of the creditors of such regulated entity.

children.heading: Maximum liability
children.label: 2 Maximum liability
children.text: The maximum liability of the Agency, acting as receiver or in any other capacity, to any person having a claim against the receiver or the regulated entity for which such receiver is appointed shall be not more than the amount that such claimant would have received if the Agency had liquidated the assets and liabilities of the regulated entity without exercising the authority of the Agency under subsection (i).

children.heading: Limitation on court action
children.label: f Limitation on court action
children.text: Except as provided in this section or at the request of the Director, no court may take any action to restrain or affect the exercise of powers or functions of the Agency as a conservator or a receiver.

children.heading: Liability of directors and officers
children.label: g Liability of directors and officers
A
children.text: acting as conservator or receiver of such regulated entity; or

children.label: B
children.text: acting based upon a suit, claim, or cause of action purchased from, assigned by, or otherwise conveyed by such receiver or conservator.

children.heading: Actions addressed
children.label: 2 Actions addressed
children.text: Paragraph (1) applies in any civil action for gross negligence, including any similar conduct or conduct that demonstrates a greater disregard of a duty of care than gross negligence, including intentional tortious conduct, as such terms are defined and determined under applicable State law.

children.heading: No limitation
children.label: 3 No limitation
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall impair or affect any right of the Agency under other applicable law.

children.heading: Damages
children.label: h Damages
children.text: In any proceeding related to any claim against a director, officer, employee, agent, attorney, accountant, appraiser, or any other party employed by or providing services to a regulated entity, recoverable damages determined to result from the improvident or otherwise improper use or investment of any assets of the regulated entity shall include principal losses and appropriate interest.

children.label: i
children.text: may, in the case of a Federal Home Loan Bank, organize a limited-life regulated entity with those powers and attributes of the Federal Home Loan Bank in default or in danger of default as the Director determines necessary, subject to the provisions of this subsection, and the Director shall grant a temporary charter to that limited-life regulated entity, and that limited-life regulated entity may operate subject to that charter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall, in the case of an enterprise, organize a limited-life regulated entity with respect to that enterprise in accordance with this subsection.

children.label: i
children.text: assume such liabilities of the regulated entity that is in default or in danger of default as the Agency may, in its discretion, determine to be appropriate, except that the liabilities assumed shall not exceed the amount of assets purchased or transferred from the regulated entity to the limited-life regulated entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: purchase such assets of the regulated entity that is in default, or in danger of default as the Agency may, in its discretion, determine to be appropriate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: perform any other temporary function which the Agency may, in its discretion, prescribe in accordance with this section.

children.label: I
children.text: succeed to the charter of the Federal National Mortgage Association, as set forth in the Federal National Mortgage Association Charter Act [ et seq.]; and12 U.S.C. 1716

children.label: II
children.text: thereafter operate in accordance with, and subject to, such charter, this Act, and any other provision of law to which the Federal National Mortgage Association is subject, except as otherwise provided in this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: succeed to the charter of the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation, as set forth in the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation Charter Act [ et seq.]; and112 U.S.C. 1451

children.label: II
children.text: thereafter operate in accordance with, and subject to, such charter, this Act, and any other provision of law to which the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation is subject, except as otherwise provided in this subsection.

children.heading: Interests in and assets and obligations of regulated entity in default
children.label: B Interests in and assets and obligations of regulated entity in default
i
children.text: a limited-life regulated entity shall assume, acquire, or succeed to the assets or liabilities of a regulated entity only to the extent that such assets or liabilities are transferred by the Agency to the limited-life regulated entity in accordance with, and subject to the restrictions set forth in, paragraph (1)(B);

children.label: ii
children.text: a limited-life regulated entity shall not assume, acquire, or succeed to any obligation that a regulated entity for which a receiver has been appointed may have to any shareholder of the regulated entity that arises as a result of the status of that person as a shareholder of the regulated entity; and

children.label: iii
children.text: no shareholder or creditor of a regulated entity shall have any right or claim against the charter of the regulated entity once the Agency has been appointed receiver for the regulated entity and a limited-life regulated entity succeeds to the charter pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Limited-life regulated entity treated as being in default for certain purposes
children.label: C Limited-life regulated entity treated as being in default for certain purposes
children.text: A limited-life regulated entity shall be treated as a regulated entity in default at such times and for such purposes as the Agency may, in its discretion, determine.

children.heading: Management
children.label: D Management
children.text: Upon its establishment, a limited-life regulated entity shall be under the management of a board of directors consisting of not fewer than 5 nor more than 10 members appointed by the Agency.

children.heading: Bylaws
children.label: E Bylaws
children.text: The board of directors of a limited-life regulated entity shall adopt such bylaws as may be approved by the Agency.

children.heading: No agency requirement
children.label: A No agency requirement
children.text: The Agency is not required to pay capital stock into a limited-life regulated entity or to issue any capital stock on behalf of a limited-life regulated entity established under this subsection.

children.heading: Authority
children.label: B Authority
children.text: If the Director determines that such action is advisable, the Agency may cause capital stock or other securities of a limited-life regulated entity established with respect to an enterprise to be issued and offered for sale, in such amounts and on such terms and conditions as the Director may determine, in the discretion of the Director.

children.heading: Investments
children.label: 4 Investments
children.text: Funds of a limited-life regulated entity shall be kept on hand in cash, invested in obligations of the United States or obligations guaranteed as to principal and interest by the United States, or deposited with the Agency, or any Federal reserve bank.

children.heading: Exempt tax status
children.label: 5 Exempt tax status
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of Federal or State law, a limited-life regulated entity, its franchise, property, and income shall be exempt from all taxation now or hereafter imposed by the United States, by any territory, dependency, or possession thereof, or by any State, county, municipality, or local taxing authority.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraphs (B) and (C), not later than 2 years after the date of its organization, the Agency shall wind up the affairs of a limited-life regulated entity.

children.heading: Extension
children.label: B Extension
children.text: The Director may, in the discretion of the Director, extend the status of a limited-life regulated entity for 3 additional 1-year periods.

children.heading: Termination of status as limited-life regulated entity
children.label: C Termination of status as limited-life regulated entity
I
children.text: the status of the limited-life regulated entity as such shall terminate; and

children.label: II
children.text: the entity shall cease to be a limited-life regulated entity for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Divestiture of remaining stock, if any
children.label: ii Divestiture of remaining stock, if any

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the date on which the status of a limited-life regulated entity is terminated pursuant to clause (i), the Agency shall sell to 1 or more persons (other than the Agency) any remaining capital stock of the former limited-life regulated entity.

children.heading: Extension authorized
children.label: II Extension authorized
children.text: The Director may extend the period referred to in subclause (I) for not longer than an additional 2 years, if the Director determines that such action would be in the public interest.

children.heading: Savings clause
children.label: iii Savings clause
