children.text: Notwithstanding any provision of law, other than clause (ii), the Agency shall not be required to sell the capital stock of an enterprise or a limited-life regulated entity established with respect to an enterprise.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: iv Applicability
children.text: This subparagraph applies only with respect to a limited-life regulated entity that is established with respect to an enterprise.

children.heading: Transfer of assets and liabilities
children.label: i Transfer of assets and liabilities
children.text: The Agency, as receiver, may transfer any assets and liabilities of a regulated entity in default, or in danger of default, to the limited-life regulated entity in accordance with and subject to the restrictions of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Subsequent transfers
children.label: ii Subsequent transfers
children.text: At any time after the establishment of a limited-life regulated entity, the Agency, as receiver, may transfer any assets and liabilities of the regulated entity in default, or in danger of default, as the Agency may, in its discretion, determine to be appropriate in accordance with and subject to the restrictions of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Effective without approval
children.label: iii Effective without approval
children.text: The transfer of any assets or liabilities of a regulated entity in default or in danger of default to a limited-life regulated entity shall be effective without any further approval under Federal or State law, assignment, or consent with respect thereto.

children.heading: Equitable treatment of similarly situated creditors
children.label: iv Equitable treatment of similarly situated creditors
I
children.text: the Director determines that such actions are necessary to maximize the value of the assets of the regulated entity, to maximize the present value return from the sale or other disposition of the assets of the regulated entity, or to minimize the amount of any loss realized upon the sale or other disposition of the assets of the regulated entity; and

children.label: II
children.text: all creditors that are similarly situated under subsection (c)(1) receive not less than the amount provided in subsection (e)(2).

children.heading: Limitation on transfer of liabilities
children.label: v Limitation on transfer of liabilities
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the aggregate amount of liabilities of a regulated entity that are transferred to, or assumed by, a limited-life regulated entity may not exceed the aggregate amount of assets of the regulated entity that are transferred to, or purchased by, the limited-life regulated entity.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 8 Regulations
children.text: The Agency may promulgate such regulations as the Agency determines to be necessary or appropriate to implement this subsection.

children.label: I
children.text: remove the directors of a limited-life regulated entity;

children.label: II
children.text: fix the compensation of members of the board of directors and senior management, as determined by the Agency in its discretion, of a limited-life regulated entity; and

children.label: III
children.text: indemnify the representatives for purposes of paragraph (1)(B), and the directors, officers, employees, and agents of a limited-life regulated entity on such terms as the Agency determines to be appropriate; and

children.label: I
children.text: shall elect a chairperson who may also serve in the position of chief executive officer, except that such person shall not serve either as chairperson or as chief executive officer without the prior approval of the Agency; and

children.label: II
children.text: may appoint a chief executive officer who is not also the chairperson, except that such person shall not serve as chief executive officer without the prior approval of the Agency.

children.heading: Stay of judicial action
children.label: B Stay of judicial action
children.text: Any judicial action to which a limited-life regulated entity becomes a party by virtue of its acquisition of any assets or assumption of any liabilities of a regulated entity in default shall be stayed from further proceedings for a period of not longer than 45 days, at the request of the limited-life regulated entity. Such period may be modified upon the consent of all parties.

children.heading: Agency status
children.label: A Agency status
children.text: A limited-life regulated entity is not an agency, establishment, or instrumentality of the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: solely by virtue of service in any such capacity lose any existing status as an officer or employee of the United States for purposes of title 5 or any other provision of law; or

children.label: ii
children.text: receive any salary or benefits for service in any such capacity with respect to a limited-life regulated entity in addition to such salary or benefits as are obtained through employment with the Agency or such Federal instrumentality.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A limited-life regulated entity may obtain unsecured credit and issue unsecured debt.

children.label: i
children.text: with priority over any or all of the obligations of the limited-life regulated entity;

children.label: ii
children.text: secured by a lien on property of the limited-life regulated entity that is not otherwise subject to a lien; or

children.label: iii
children.text: secured by a junior lien on property of the limited-life regulated entity that is subject to a lien.

children.label: i In general3So in original. No cl. (ii) has been enacted.3
I
children.text: the limited-life regulated entity is unable to otherwise obtain such credit or issue such debt; and

children.label: II
children.text: there is adequate protection of the interest of the holder of the lien on the property with respect to which such senior or equal lien is proposed to be granted.

children.heading: Burden of proof
children.label: D Burden of proof
children.text: In any hearing under this subsection, the Director has the burden of proof on the issue of adequate protection.

children.heading: Effect on debts and liens
children.label: 12 Effect on debts and liens
children.text: The reversal or modification on appeal of an authorization under this subsection to obtain credit or issue debt, or of a grant under this section of a priority or a lien, does not affect the validity of any debt so issued, or any priority or lien so granted, to an entity that extended such credit in good faith, whether or not such entity knew of the pendency of the appeal, unless such authorization and the issuance of such debt, or the granting of such priority or lien, were stayed pending appeal.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: 1 Applicability
children.text: The provisions of this subsection shall apply with respect to the Agency in any case in which the Agency is acting as a conservator or a receiver.

children.heading: Taxation
children.label: 2 Taxation
children.text: The Agency, including its franchise, its capital, reserves, and surplus, and its income, shall be exempt from all taxation imposed by any State, county, municipality, or local taxing authority, except that any real property of the Agency shall be subject to State, territorial, county, municipal, or local taxation to the same extent according to its value as other real property is taxed, except that, notwithstanding the failure of any person to challenge an assessment under State law of the value of such property, and the tax thereon, shall be determined as of the period for which such tax is imposed.

children.heading: Property protection
children.label: 3 Property protection
children.text: No property of the Agency shall be subject to levy, attachment, garnishment, foreclosure, or sale without the consent of the Agency, nor shall any involuntary lien attach to the property of the Agency.

children.heading: Penalties and fines
children.label: 4 Penalties and fines
children.text: The Agency shall not be liable for any amounts in the nature of penalties or fines, including those arising from the failure of any person to pay any real property, personal property, probate, or recording tax or any recording or filing fees when due.

children.heading: Prohibition of charter revocation
children.label: k Prohibition of charter revocation
children.text: In no case may the receiver appointed pursuant to this section revoke, annul, or terminate the charter of an enterprise.
